attempted_route: Built (64-syllogism x vocab) multi-hot matrix per model CSV, flattened, used align_vector() to zero-pad/truncate to W.flatten().size (1152), then np.dot cosine similarity against w_con_flat and w_ope_flat. No sklearn used.
observation: exec exit_code=0, output CSV written; but eval score=8/14. Script ran without error yet produced partially wrong similarity values due to semantic space mismatch hidden by zero-padding.
rationale: align_vector() zero-pads a (64 x vocab)-flattened vector to 1152 dims, but W shape is (n_factors x n_syllogisms), a different semantic space. Cosine is computed between misaligned spaces; round-1 assumed same-space once dims matched, which is incorrect for JNMF W matrices.
recommended_alternative: Determine W shape (e.g. n_factors x 64) via W_con.shape. Build model matrix as (64 x n_responses) probability or multi-hot, then project into factor space via W_con @ model_matrix.T or np.linalg.lstsq. Compute cosine between projected vectors, no padding.
